title: centaurea athoa subsp. athoa
intro: Centaurea athoa subsp. athoa is a subspecies of the annual or biennial plant, Athoa, also known as Mountain Cornflower, which is native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits associated with Centaurea athoa subsp. athoa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
